series_metadata.series_description: Cost allocation plans and related correspondence to and from the federalgovernment detailing operating expenditures of the agency.All active records identified as being needed by the Ohio Department of Medicaid (ODM) for their daily operations as of July 1, 2013, were transferred by the Ohio Department of Job and Family Services (ODJFS) to ODM. Any records that are inactive and identified as being not necessary for daily operations by ODM were retained by ODJFS.

retention_rules.disposition: Microform: Retain 3 years in office, then microfilm. Destroy paper after microfilm is verified. Retain in office 10 years, until federal and state audits have been conducted, audit reports released, and audit resolution issued or resolved. Duplicate set of microfilm to be stored at the Records Center and destroyed as above. Microfilming was discontinued in 2000. ; Electronic: Retain until no longer of administrative value, and then destroy. ; Paper: Retain in office 3 years and 4 years in the records center, then destroy provided audits have been completed and released, and all discrepancies have been resolved., Microform: Shred ; Electronic: Erase ; Paper: Shred
